passage: [Figure: two right triangles, PQR and STU, with right angles marked at R and U. Note: figures not drawn to scale.]
stem: Right triangles PQR and STU are similar, where P corresponds to S. If the measure of angle Q is 18°, what is the measure of angle S?
explanation: In the similar triangles, the right angles are at R and U, so Q corresponds to T and P corresponds to S. Angles Q and T are congruent, so angle T = 18°; then in triangle STU, angle S = 180° − 90° − 18° = 72°.
